page: OVHcloud t1-le-180 is a GPU-accelerated server instance powered by an Intel Xeon Silver 4214 processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 3.0 GHz with one thread per core, supported by a KVM hypervisor. The hardware configuration includes 180.0 GB of system memory, providing 5.62 GB per core. For acceleration, the server is equipped with four NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs, delivering a total of 64 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface provides a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear division between CPU and GPU capabilities. Single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 25% across stress-ng, gzip, and bzip3 tests. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations score in the middle 50% tier. The system excels in parallelized workloads, particularly LLM inference. Prompt processing and text generation for medium (7B) and large (70B) models rank in the top 10% tier, while text generation for small (135M) models falls into the top 25% tier.

The primary tradeoff of the t1-le-180 is its modest single-core CPU performance relative to its high-density GPU and memory resources. This makes it less suitable for single-threaded compute tasks. Qualitatively, the server offers a specialized hardware profile where the cost is concentrated in the quad-GPU setup and high memory capacity rather than raw CPU clock speed. It is best suited for GPU-intensive workloads such as large language model inference, machine learning training, data science pipelines, and parallelized multi-threaded data processing.
description: OVHcloud t1-le-180 is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the x86_64 architecture with a KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores from an Intel Xeon Silver 4214 processor running at 3.0 GHz, paired with 180.0 GB of system memory. The server's primary strength lies in its graphics hardware, which consists of four NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs with a total of 64 GB of VRAM. Storage is provided by a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is rated at a 10 Gbps baseline. While single-core CPU benchmarks are in the bottom 25%, multi-core performance is average, and LLM inference benchmarks for small, medium, and large models rank in the top 10% to 25% tiers. This hardware profile is highly optimized for parallelized workloads, making it a strong fit for machine learning inference, data science, and multi-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud t1-le-180 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 180 GB RAM, 400 GB NVMe storage, and four NVIDIA V100 GPUs with 64 GB VRAM, optimized for high-tier LLM inference.
meta_description: OVHcloud t1-le-180 is a GPU-accelerated server with 32 Intel Xeon cores, 180 GB RAM, and four NVIDIA V100 GPUs, built for LLM inference workloads.
tagline: A quad-GPU accelerated server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and high-capacity VRAM for demanding parallel computing and machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs providing 64 GB of total VRAM
32 dedicated physical Intel Xeon cores running at 3.0 GHz
180 GB of system memory and 400 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Optimized for machine learning, data science, and parallelized workloads
